name: K8s troubleshooter — evaluator deep-dive (real cluster)
description: Same scenarios as demo 1, scored with three different custom-evaluator architectures (Python LLM judge, JavaScript stdin/stdout, OpenAI Evals API delegation) plus a built-in trajectory check. The point is to demonstrate the plugin contract across language runtimes — not to score the agent harder. Run against the real broken-workloads.yaml cluster.

parts.text: The nginx pods in the prod namespace are crashlooping. What's wrong?

The nginx Deployment pods are in CrashLoopBackOff because the liveness probe at /healthz returns HTTP 404 (the default nginx image does not serve a /healthz path), and the mounted nginx config in /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf references an upstream `backend-svc` that does not exist. Fix: either replace the liveness probe with a path nginx actually serves (e.g. `/`) or remove the bad upstream block.
Why can't the api Deployment's pods start in the prod namespace?

The api Deployment's pods are stuck in ImagePullBackOff. The describe events show kubelet failing to pull `ghcr.io/acme/api:v1.7.3-does-not-exist` — the tag does not exist in the registry. Fix: update the image tag in the Deployment to a tag that actually exists.
